summary: Fix option to "Search for unlinked local files" for new library

description.text:
Disables the option to "Search for unlinked local files" in the Lookup menu before the newly created library is saved as the action should only be available when the .bib file path is known.

I changed the executable property binding to needsSavedLocalDatabase() to ensure that the action is only available under the defined conditions of the mentioned method.
I developed tests that assert that the action is only executable under those same conditions.

The video below shows the creation of a new library (without saving it) and how the "Search for unliked local files" is now disabled.

[bug_fix.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d3e7299-fc8d-4ea7-83c7-fd0326d878c0)

Fixes #12558

summary: Keyboard shortcuts for fetching bibliographic data using identifier

description.text:
Fixes #11802 
Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/8335
Resolves https://discourse.jabref.org/t/shortcuts-for-get-bibliographic-data-from-doi-and-look-up-doi/4468


This PR introduces keyboard shortcuts for the following actions:
- Get bibliographic data from DOI
- Look up DOI

summary: "Reveal in file explorer" should be enabled after save

description.text:
1. Create new library
2. Save
3. Right click on tab heading
4. See "Reveal in file explorer" is disabled\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b405c866-1a34-478f-a20b-ae4c33495b49)

At step 4, it should be enabled.

1. Close the bib file
2. Re-open it
3. See that "Reveal in file explorer" is available.

summary: Group search should be OR - not AND

description.text:
Context: JabRef groups - see https://docs.jabref.org/finding-sorting-and-cleaning-entries/groups for a longer explanation.

One can search for groups in the side pane. When entering two group names separated by a space, JabRef should match group strings containing any of these words. (Currently, it requires ALL words to be present)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/429cc46c-d64c-4100-866d-a33039d0287e)

Task: 

- Investigate if [`Search.g4`](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/8547513eea1f91cbf77794180901b1b78a9d9535/jablib/src/main/antlr/org/jabref/search/Search.g4#L4) can be used here, too.
- Re-implement the search using that grammar here

Refs https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/10209 somehow

summary: Triangle moves around in "Search for unlinked local files" dialog

description.text:
### JabRef version

6.0-alpha

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

Arch Linux, MATE DE

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Open "Search for unlinked files dialog".
2. Perform search

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/beb4460c-3790-460b-891f-fb6ae3c9797e)

3. Import some files.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7c6ddff-9dc3-4b14-bffe-33c982416192)

You see, triangle is that the beginning of the label "Import result".

--

If I click again on "Search results", it's still there:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/092525d1-2b65-4b7c-ab12-01d4339c17e3)

If I click back again at "Import result", then it works okayish:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f9e71970-01b3-4bad-9202-f119dda9f13e)



### Appendix

_No response_

Unlinked files dialog is now a simple dialog that makes the user work step by step. select library, select directory, select file extensions to look for. Then search for files. Then select from the search results the files to import.

This could be improved to a proper assistant. ControlsFX offers a wizard class. https://github.com/controlsfx/controlsfx/pull/1542 should be merged soon - this is a pre-condition.

## Background

- Current dialog: https://docs.jabref.org/collect/findunlinkedfiles#using-the-wizard-search-for-unlinked-local-files
- Pattern "Assistent": http://www.usabilitypatterns.info/catalog/patterns/assistent.html

summary: Add example questions to AI chat

description.text:
**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/436e9b08-4523-4186-872e-7baebe1373f8)

JabRef features an AI chat, though it's a bit basic. Many AI services provide a feature called "example questions". This is a fixed set of questions that a user can ask at the beginning of chat.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Add 3 example questions in the AI chat above the text field:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2ce1ff9c-80cf-4efb-899a-607a7a7ba617)

- `What is the goal of the paper?`
- `Which methods were used in the research?`
- `What are the key findings?`

Add an `HBox` with those questions just above the chat prompt (<https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/4dea5fae711f9dd61c94f6a170b475b9e0633e22/src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/ai/components/aichat/AiChatComponent.fxml#L25-L24>). Make them hyperlinks, and on clicking, it should send a message to AI chat (org.jabref.gui.ai.components.aichat.AiChatComponent#onSendMessage).

**Additional context**

[N/A]

description.text:
The dialog filters on the type

Then the columns with the same content should be removed.

summary: Check consistency should have a progress bar

description.text:
1. Open a large library
2. Do Quality -> Check integrity
3. A progress is displayed (see https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12694)
4. Close the dialog
5. Do Quality -> Check consistency
6. No dialog is displayed

With: Progresds dialog is displayed

The same stirng appears at two places:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3b3c144-de03-4f76-971d-dd5b2fae44be)

Doesn't look professional.

Remove the yellow string.

summary: No tab shown when untitled tab is created

description.text:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

_No response_

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Set preference to true for "Hide tab bar when single library is present"
2. Create a new library
3. Change the preference to false.
4. Now in tab bar no tab is shown.
